title: [FIRRTL] (connect) 0-width error. LowerToRTL couldn't handle this operation
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module mod_2 :
    input inp0: UInt<0>
  module top_mod :
    inst U0 of mod_2
    U0 is invalid
```

Compiled with `firtool --lower-to-rtl --enable-lower-types` gives an error:

```mlir
a_top_mod.fir:6:5: error: 'firrtl.connect' op LowerToRTL couldn't handle this operation
    U0 is invalid
    ^
a_top_mod.fir:6:5: note: see current operation: "firrtl.connect"(%.inp0.wire, %0) : (!firrtl.uint<0>, !firrtl.uint<0>) -> ()
a_top_mod.fir:6:5: error: 'comb.constant' op result #0 must be an integer bitvector of one or more bits, but got 'i0'
    U0 is invalid
    ^
a_top_mod.fir:6:5: note: see current operation: %c0_i0 = "comb.constant"() {value = 0 : i0} : () -> i0
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5.0` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
);
endmodule
```
